findings.description: Docstring claims strict YYYY-MM-DD parsing, but strptime with %Y-%m-%d also accepts non-zero-padded values like '2024-1-1', so 'strictly' overstates the contract.
clamp_to_range does not validate tz-awareness of value/lower/upper; mixed naive/aware datetimes make min/max raise TypeError instead of the behavior implied by the docstring.
business_days_between materializes the entire day_range list just to count weekdays, using O(n) memory for what could be an O(1)-memory count.
day_range eagerly allocates a full list, so adversarially large spans (e.g., centuries) cause large memory allocations with no upper bound.
parse_iso_date raises TypeError (not the documented ValueError) when given None or non-str input, since type hints are not enforced at runtime.
